title: Rose Byrne Wins Golden Globe for If I Had Legs I'd Kick You
text: Rose Byrne claimed victory at the 2026 Golden Globes for her performance in writer-director Mary Bronstein's indie film "If I Had Legs I'd Kick You." The win represents recognition for a challenging character-driven narrative centered on an ordinary suburban mother facing personal crisis. The movie follows Linda, a therapist caring for a daughter with a mysterious illness, as her life unravels. Byrne describes the narrative as containing significant emotional intensity: "There's a fury in the movie. There's a fury behind the character, but she has no ability." Byrne underwent extensive preparation with Bronstein, meeting three days weekly for five weeks to analyze the script page-by-page. The actress also consulted with mothers of children with special needs and engaged in specialized breathwork training to authentically portray the character's psychological state. The ensemble includes Conan O'Brien as Linda's therapist, Christian Slater as her largely absent husband (primarily heard via phone), A$AP Rocky in a supporting role, and Danielle MacDonald as the unseen daughter's voice. Byrne notes the significance of seeing complex female characters anchoring films through a distinctly female creative perspective, comparing the work's thematic depth to classic cinema traditions.
